markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Feed code symbol neighborhoods into bounded context bundles with citations and token accounting. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: CodeContextBundleBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CodeContextBundleBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commits 5d6490b0 (compiling foundation) + 79e1a7e4 (runtime proof); 43 lib + 11 integration + 1 nav-API tests green on real PostgreSQL under runtime-full,test-utils. Found+fixed impl-Type/struct-Type entity collision. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: OUTPUT bounded, WORK unbounded: the token budget caps how many neighbors are EMITTED, but build_code_context_bundle first fetches ALL references edges of the focus (context_bridge.rs:119 list_knowledge_edges_for_entity, no cap) and does a get_knowledge_entity + first_ast_span round-trip for EVERY neighbor (context_bridge.rs:134-141) BEFORE the budget truncation runs (context_bridge.rs:190). A hot symbol called by 10k sites does 10k+ DB queries and builds 10k CitedNeighbor structs, then throws most away. The pathological symbol graph the attack posits DOES blow the WORK budget (DB + memory), even though the model's context window is protected. The budget is an output guardrail, not a work guardrail.

Focus always emitted in full regardless of budget: used = focus_tokens.min(budget) (context_bridge.rs:179) clamps the accounting, but the focus's display_name/key/doc are written into allowed_context unconditionally (context_bridge.rs:231-244). If focus_doc is large the bundle exceeds token_budget. Mitigated in practice because doc display_name is truncated to ~120 chars at ingestion (truncate_snippet), but the budget is not actually enforced on the focus.

Token estimate is chars/4 (context_bridge.rs:40) and counts only display_name + entity_key per neighbor, NOT the actual snippet/source text (the bundle carries no source text, only keys+line ranges). So 'estimated_tokens' undercounts what a consumer that later fetches the spans will spend - the budget protects the bundle JSON size, not the eventual hydrated context. Honest as a guardrail but the name overstates it.
Recursion: NONE - only direct calls/called_by neighbors are included; no transitive expansion. The unbounded-recursion probe is refuted.
evasion_probes: Confirmed single-hop neighborhood only (context_bridge.rs:121-160): edges touching focus, relation calls/called_by, no recursive descent into neighbors. Cannot recurse unboundedly.

Confirmed deterministic packing: neighbors sorted, dedup by (relation,key), packed smallest-first within budget, truncated flag recorded in token_accounting (context_bridge.rs:247-254). A re-run yields the same bundle.
Confirmed staleness is attached (context_bridge.rs:105 file_staleness -> staleness_label in allowed_context) so a stale neighborhood is labeled.
Confirmed the bundle is the kernel's existing durable ContextBundle (ContextBundle::new) and the module writes no rows itself - matches the 'projection only' constraint.
evidence_checked: delegate tests: token estimate determinism + cited-neighbor json (logic-only, no large-fanout test)

hardening_pending.reason: MEDIUM: output bounded but WORK unbounded (fetches ALL references edges before budget); focus always emitted full; token estimate undercounts.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
